name: Colosseum
description: The largest ancient amphitheatre ever built, completed in 80 AD. Held 50,000-80,000 spectators watching gladiatorial contests.

name: Pantheon
description: Best-preserved ancient Roman building with its massive concrete dome and oculus. Built as a temple to all gods.

name: Roman Forum
description: The political and commercial center of ancient Rome for over a millennium.

name: Pompeii
description: Roman city preserved by volcanic ash from Mount Vesuvius eruption in 79 AD.

name: Vatican Museums
description: One of the world's greatest art collections spanning 42 galleries including the Sistine Chapel.

name: Sistine Chapel
description: Michelangelo's ceiling masterpiece depicting the Creation of Adam. Site of papal conclaves.

name: St. Peter's Basilica
description: The largest church in the world, built over Saint Peter's tomb. Features Michelangelo's Pietà.

name: Florence Cathedral
description: Crowned by Brunelleschi's dome, the largest masonry dome ever built.

name: Ponte Vecchio
description: Oldest bridge in Florence, lined with jewelry shops since 1593.

name: Uffizi Gallery
description: One of the world's finest art museums with works by Botticelli, Leonardo, and Raphael.

name: Leaning Tower of Pisa
description: Famous bell tower that tilts 3.97 degrees due to unstable foundation soil.

name: Grand Canal
description: The major water-traffic corridor in Venice, lined with over 170 magnificent buildings.

name: St. Mark's Basilica
description: Venice's most famous church with 8,000 sq meters of golden Byzantine mosaics.

name: Amalfi Coast
description: Dramatic coastline with colorful villages perched on cliffs above the Mediterranean.

name: Eiffel Tower
description: Iconic wrought-iron tower built for the 1889 World's Fair. At 330m, it was the world's tallest for 41 years.

name: Louvre Museum
description: World's largest art museum with 380,000 works including Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo.

name: Palace of Versailles
description: Louis XIV's opulent palace with the Hall of Mirrors, 700 rooms, and 800 hectares of gardens.

name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
description: French Gothic masterpiece with flying buttresses, rose windows, and gargoyles. Reopened 2024 after fire.

name: Mont Saint-Michel
description: Gravity-defying abbey on a tidal island. One of the first pilgrimage sites of the Way of St. James.

name: Palais des Papes
description: Largest Gothic palace in Europe, served as papal residence in the 14th century.

name: Château de Chambord
description: Largest château in the Loire Valley with a famous double-helix staircase possibly designed by da Vinci.

name: Alhambra
description: Nasrid dynasty palace with intricate tilework, stucco carvings, and water gardens.

name: Sagrada Família
description: Gaudí's unfinished masterpiece basilica combining Gothic and Art Nouveau forms.

name: Park Güell
description: Whimsical public park by Gaudí with mosaic sculptures and panoramic city views.

name: Alcázar of Seville
description: Royal palace blending Moorish and Christian architecture. Used as Dorne in Game of Thrones.

name: Aqueduct of Segovia
description: Well-preserved Roman aqueduct with 167 arches stretching 818 meters.

name: Santiago Cathedral
description: Final destination of the Camino pilgrimage, believed to house Saint James's remains.

name: Stonehenge
description: Prehistoric stone circle built 3000-2000 BC. Purpose debated: observatory, ritual site, or healing center.

name: Tower of London
description: 900-year-old fortress housing the Crown Jewels and Yeoman Warders.

name: Buckingham Palace
description: Official London residence of the British monarch since 1837 with 775 rooms.

name: Big Ben & Parliament
description: The iconic clock tower at the Houses of Parliament, officially renamed Elizabeth Tower in 2012.

name: Windsor Castle
description: Oldest occupied castle in the world, royal residence for 900+ years.

name: Roman Baths
description: Remarkably preserved Roman bathing complex built around natural hot springs.

name: Edinburgh Castle
description: Fortress on Castle Rock, royal residence for 1,000+ years. Houses Scottish Crown Jewels.

name: Neuschwanstein Castle
description: Fairy-tale castle that inspired Disney's Sleeping Beauty Castle.

name: Cologne Cathedral
description: Gothic masterpiece with 157m twin spires. Took 600 years to complete.

name: Brandenburg Gate
description: Iconic symbol of German reunification, once part of the Berlin Wall.

name: Berlin Wall Memorial
description: Preserved section of the Berlin Wall with documentation center.

name: Burg Eltz
description: Hidden hilltop castle, never destroyed in 850 years, still owned by the original family.

name: Acropolis of Athens
description: Ancient citadel crowned by the Parthenon, symbol of democracy and classical civilization.

name: Parthenon
description: Temple dedicated to Athena with perfect proportions that influenced Western architecture.

name: Delphi
description: Sacred sanctuary where the Oracle of Apollo prophesied. Considered the center of the world.

name: Mycenae
description: Bronze Age fortress city, home of Agamemnon. Features the Lion Gate and royal tombs.

name: Hagia Sophia
description: Marvel of Byzantine engineering with massive dome, served as church and mosque for 1,500 years.

name: Blue Mosque
description: Famous for 20,000 İznik tiles and six minarets, built to rival Hagia Sophia.

name: Topkapi Palace
description: Primary residence of Ottoman sultans for 400 years, housing the Spoonmaker's Diamond.

name: Ephesus
description: Ancient Greek city, home to the Temple of Artemis. One of the best-preserved ancient cities.

name: Cappadocia
description: Surreal landscape of fairy chimneys and cave dwellings carved by volcanic erosion.

name: Schönbrunn Palace
description: Habsburg summer residence with 1,441 rooms and Europe's oldest zoo.

name: St. Stephen's Cathedral
description: Vienna's most important Gothic building with its distinctive multicolored tile roof.

name: Hallstatt
description: Picturesque lakeside village with 7,000-year salt mining history, inspired Frozen's Arendelle.

name: Prague Castle
description: Largest ancient castle complex in the world, covering 70,000 sq meters.

name: Charles Bridge
description: Historic bridge lined with 30 baroque statues, connecting Old Town to the Castle.

name: Dubrovnik Old Town
description: 'Pearl of the Adriatic' with perfectly preserved medieval walls and baroque buildings.

name: Pena Palace
description: Kaleidoscopic hilltop palace blending Gothic, Moorish, and Renaissance styles.

name: Belém Tower

name: Amsterdam Canals
description: 165 bridges and 100km of canals forming the UNESCO-protected Canal Ring.

name: Anne Frank House
description: The secret annex where Anne Frank hid from the Nazis and wrote her famous diary.

name: Urnes Stave Church
description: Oldest surviving stave church in Norway, dating to 1130 with Viking art carvings.

name: North Cape
description: Europe's northernmost point with dramatic cliffs rising 307m above the Arctic Ocean.

name: Vaduz Castle
description: Official residence of the Prince of Liechtenstein, perched above the capital.

name: Kilkenny Castle
description: Striking castle overlooking the River Nore, built after the Norman invasion.

name: Clonmacnoise
description: Monastic site founded in 544 AD with high crosses, round towers, and Celtic manuscripts.

name: Gamla Uppsala
description: Ancient Viking royal burial mounds dating to the 6th century.

name: Drottningholm Palace
description: Swedish royal family's private residence, the Versailles of Scandinavia.

name: Gjerdrum landslide area
description: Site of the 2020 quick clay landslide, showing nature's power.

name: Bran Castle
description: Known as 'Dracula's Castle', perched on a cliff between Transylvania and Wallachia.

name: Peleș Castle
description: Stunning Neo-Renaissance castle in the Carpathian Mountains, former royal summer residence.

name: Buda Castle
description: Royal palace complex on Castle Hill overlooking the Danube.

name: Hungarian Parliament
description: Gothic Revival masterpiece on the Danube, housing the Holy Crown of Hungary.

name: Wawel Castle
description: Royal castle on the Vistula, residence of Polish kings for centuries.

name: Wieliczka Salt Mine
description: Ancient salt mine with underground chapels carved from salt, including the Chapel of St. Kinga.

name: Auschwitz-Birkenau
description: Former Nazi concentration and extermination camp, now a memorial and museum.

name: Moscow Kremlin
description: Historic fortified complex at the heart of Moscow, official residence of the President of Russia.

name: Red Square
description: Moscow's iconic square surrounded by St. Basil's, the Kremlin, and the GUM department store.

name: St. Basil's Cathedral
description: Iconic cathedral with colorful onion domes, built by Ivan the Terrible.

name: Hermitage Museum
description: One of the world's oldest and largest art museums with over 3 million items.

name: Monte Cassino

name: Fushimi Inari Shrine
description: Famous for thousands of vermillion torii gates forming tunnels up the sacred mountain.

name: Kinkaku-ji
description: Golden pavilion covered in gold leaf, reflecting in its mirror pond.

name: Tōdai-ji
description: Houses the world's largest bronze Buddha (15m) in the world's largest wooden building.

name: Hiroshima Peace Memorial
description: The Genbaku Dome, preserved in its post-bombing state as a symbol of peace.

name: Itsukushima Shrine
description: Famous 'floating' torii gate standing in the water at high tide.

name: Himeji Castle
description: Japan's most spectacular castle, known as 'White Heron Castle' for its white exterior.

name: Osaka Castle
description: Iconic castle built by Toyotomi Hideyoshi, symbol of Japanese unification.

name: Sensō-ji

name: Great Wall of China
description: Ancient fortification stretching over 21,000 km, the longest structure ever built.

name: Forbidden City
description: World's largest palace complex with 980 buildings, home to 24 emperors.

name: Terracotta Army

name: Summer Palace
description: Largest preserved imperial garden in China with Kunming Lake and Longevity Hill.

name: Jiuzhaigou Valley
description: Fairytale landscape of multicolored lakes, waterfalls, and snow-capped peaks.

name: Zhangjiajie National Park

name: Li River
description: Dramatic karst mountain landscape along the Li River, immortalized in Chinese paintings.

name: Potala Palace

name: Yellow Mountain
description: Famous for twisted pines, granite peaks, hot springs, and seas of clouds.

name: Angkor Wat
description: Largest religious monument, originally Hindu then Buddhist temple.

name: Angkor Thom

name: Grand Palace
nameLocal: พระบรมมหาราชวัง

name: Wat Pho

name: Bagan Temples

name: Marble Rocks

name: Hawa Mahal

name: Varanasi Ghats

name: Cape of Good Hope

name: Victoria Falls
